perturbation.original_text: 2. Purpose of the Disclosure of Confidential Information The Parties will enter into discussions regarding future collaboration toward a European Funded Project in the field of [….] or [will submit a proposal for a collaborative project in response to the call (identify the call) under (identify the EU -funded Programme)] or [wi ll enter into or evaluate alternatives for partnership or collaboration with [ name of the other Party or Parties ] for the purpose of [identify the undertaking intended to perform or achieve ].
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Purpose of the Disclosure of Confidential Information The Parties may enter into discussions.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the purpose to 'The Parties may enter into discussions' it removes the definitive purpose, which is the core reason to disclose confidential information. The lack of a defined purpose for the disclosure contradicts Section 3.1, which states that 'The Receiving Party agrees to use the Confidential Information solely in connection with purposes contemplated b e- tween the Parties in this Agreement'. This is because now the purpose becomes un undefined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 In the context of discussi ons, preparations or negotiations, the Disclosing Party may disclose Confidential Information to the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party agrees to use the Confidential Information solely in connection with purposes contemplated b e- tween the Parties in this Agreement and not to use it for any other purpose or without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines a clear purpose for disclosing confidential information. The changed text weakens this to 'may enter into discussions', effectively removing the definitive purpose. This directly contradicts Section 3.1, which requires the Receiving Party to use Confidential Information 'solely in connection with purposes contemplated between the Parties in this Agreement'. With the changed text, the purpose is now undefined, creating a contradiction as the receiving party doesn't have a defined purpose to adhere to, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. This falls under Omissions - In Text Contradiction, because it removed essential language, which creates a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduced or stored) , including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed un der this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may r equest it to the Receiving Party .
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 Upon termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party should handle Confidential Information in a responsible manner.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the specific requirements to return or destroy copies of confidential information with the vague instruction to 'handle Confidential Information in a responsible manner,' it introduces an ambiguity. This contradicts Section 3.10, which stipulates that the Disclosing Party will suffer irreparable damage if its Confidential Information is made public or disclosed and that the Disclosing Party is entitled to injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.10 The Parties agree that the Disclosing Party wi ll suffer irreparable damage if its Co n- fidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injun c- tive relief against a threatened brea ch or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such a breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the Receiving Party's obligation to return or destroy all copies of the Confidential Information upon termination of the agreement. The changed text replaces this specific obligation with a vague instruction to 'handle Confidential Information in a responsible manner.' This creates a contradiction with Section 3.10, which stipulates that the Disclosing Party will suffer irreparable damage if its Confidential Information is made public or disclosed and that the Disclosing Party is entitled to injunctive relief. The change introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects certainty, aligning with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Duration and Termination 4.1.1 This Agreeme nt shall remain in effect for a term of [number of months or years ]. Notwithstanding the foregoi ng, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Conf i- dential Information that was disclosed during the term shal l remain in effect indefinitely, save otherwise agreed.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Duration and Termination 4.1.1 This Agreement shall remain in effect until terminated by either party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause regarding the indefinite confidentiality of disclosed information after the agreement's term, while not making any other modifications to the text, it contradicts Section 3.6. By removing this indefinite obligation, the modified clause now implies that confidentiality obligations cease upon termination, regardless of existing intellectual property rights that are exclusive to the disclosing party. This would allow intellectual property to be exploited after the agreement concludes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.6 All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shal l be co n- strued as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. Principally, nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant to the Receiving Party a licence expressly or by implication under any patent, copyrigh t or other intelle c- tual property right. The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all exis t- ing and future intellectual property rights related to the Confidential Inf ormation are exclusive titles of the Disclosing Party. For the sake of cla rity based in reciprocity and good faith of the Parties, the Receiving Party will not apply for or obtain any intellectual proper ty protection in respect of the Confidential Information received. Likewise any modifications and improvements thereof by the R eceiving Party shall be the sole prope r- ty of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 4.1.1 ensured indefinite confidentiality of disclosed information, even after the agreement's term. The changed text removes this indefinite obligation, stating the agreement lasts until terminated by either party. This directly contradicts Section 3.6, which states all confidential information remains the property of the disclosing party and grants no rights to the receiving party. The removal of the indefinite confidentiality clause in Section 4.1.1 implies confidentiality obligations cease upon termination, creating a conflict with Section 3.6's assertion that all intellectual property rights remain exclusive to the disclosing party, regardless of the agreement's duration.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Applicable Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted by the laws of [choose the applicable law]. The court of [choose the jurisdiction to settle disputes ] shall have jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Applicable Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be governed by principles of good faith.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the clause specifying the applicable law and jurisdiction with a general statement that the agreement shall be governed by principles of good faith, the text introduces ambiguity and lacks legal enforceability. Section 3.10 which states 'the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injun c- tive relief against a threatened brea ch or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such a breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.' This is now contradictory due to 'good faith' lacking a clear definition or enforcement mechanism.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.10 The Parties agree that the Disclosing Party wi ll suffer irreparable damage if its Co n- fidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injun c- tive relief against a threatened brea ch or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such a breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the applicable law and jurisdiction, providing a clear basis for legal action. The changed text replaces this with a general principle of 'good faith,' which lacks a clear definition or enforcement mechanism. Section 3.10 grants the Disclosing Party the right to seek injunctive relief and damages from a 'court of competent jurisdiction.' The changed text removes the basis for determining what constitutes a 'court of competent jurisdiction' and renders the enforcement mechanism uncertain, creating a direct contradiction and impacting legal enforceability, thus causing a strong contradiction.
